t16.subject: Implement epoch-gated cache + red-green + reloc reverse index
t16.description: Upgrade peony-cache: epoch key (args_hash+mtimes), section-level diff, red-green coloring, reloc reverse index (QUAD §6, §10.2, SPEC §4 + §7.3)

t23.subject: Rocq: relocation disjoint-write + determinism
t23.description: rocq-tests/RelocDisjoint.v — footprint function, pairwise-disjoint partition theorem, parallel apply commutes = sequential (separation), determinism as confluence corollary

t18.subject: Fix section_references_moved heuristic
t18.description: Replace the 3-hardcoded-name heuristic with the actual relocation reverse index lookup, and wire compute_red_green into the driver pipeline

t8.subject: Deep paper analysis — read all 15 PDFs + blog sources
t8.description: Use haiku to read and extract key theoretical content from all PDFs in research/: dwarf5, x86-64 ABI, ELF specs, all 9 arXiv papers, mold design, Wild blog series, MaskRay posts. Produce a structured digest per paper with: key definitions, algorithms, complexity bounds, and what novel ideas they suggest for peony.

t20.subject: Linker: gcc lib-path auto-discovery
t20.description: Query `gcc -print-search-dirs` at startup, add library paths so -lgcc_s/-lc resolve without explicit -L

t9.subject: Extended research sweep — arXiv, primo, uni, dblp
t9.description: Use the Research tool + WebSearch with arXiv:/primo:/uni:/dblp:/scholar: prefixes to find papers on: incremental graph maintenance, persistent data structures for compilers, parallel hash join algorithms, delta-compressed binary patching, demand-driven dataflow, section-level liveness analysis. Download all hits to research/.

t22.subject: Rocq: GC reachability + layout congruence
t22.description: rocq-tests/SectionGC.v (level-sync BFS = reachable set) and rocq-tests/Layout.v (conditional layout existence + page-congruence invariant with bounded addresses)

t26.subject: Rocq: symbol resolution semilattice proofs
t26.description: rocq-tests/SymbolLattice.v — binding-strength join-semilattice with associativity, commutativity, idempotence; confluence of parallel resolution under fixed precedence tie-break (per council guidance)

t6.subject: Explore raddebugger codebase
t6.description: Use CodeGraph to explore /tmp/raddebugger — find RADDBG format, debug info structures, linker-relevant sections. Extract design patterns for debug info handling in peony-emit.

t10.subject: Deep codebase analysis — Wild + raddebugger
t10.description: Read Wild source excerpts (symbol_db, layout, resolution, elf_writer, save_dir, string_merging) and raddebugger RDI format headers. Extract concrete algorithms, data structure invariants, and design patterns not documented anywhere.

t11.subject: Write QUAD.md — theoretical foundations with proofs
t11.description: Synthesize all research into a formal academic document. Must include: formal definitions of the incremental linking problem, theorems on correctness of incremental reuse, complexity analysis of parallel symbol resolution, proof of determinism, novel algorithmic contributions (with pseudocode and proofs).

t17.subject: Implement S3-GC parallel BFS section GC
t17.description: Replace serial BFS in peony-layout gc_sections() with adaptive level-synchronous parallel BFS (S3-GC from QUAD §3.2 / SPEC Phase 3)

t31.subject: peony -shared: export dynsym + .hash/.gnu.hash
t31.description: In peony-layout/peony-symbols: when shared, collect all defined non-local symbols (STB_GLOBAL/STB_WEAK, defined, not hidden) and emit them as exports into .dynsym with correct st_info, st_other (visibility), st_shndx (output section index), st_value (final VA), st_size. Build real .hash (SysV) and .gnu.hash bucket/chain tables over the exports (not just imports). This is the core of making a usable .so whose symbols dlsym can find.

t34.subject: Configure cargo to use peony instead of mold
t34.description: Replace mold with peony in ~/.cargo/config.toml for x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu. Approach: install a peony 'ld' wrapper on PATH (or use -Clinker-flavor) and set rustflags to use peony as the linker driver, removing -fuse-ld=mold. Must coexist with sccache + target-cpu=native. Keep a backup of the original config. Verify a small crate (bar) builds and runs via the new global config.

t21.subject: Linker: R_X86_64_RELATIVE in .rela.dyn for PIE
t21.description: Emit R_X86_64_RELATIVE dynamic relocations + DT_RELACOUNT for absolute pointers in PIE; required for PIE data pointers

t25.subject: Linker: symbol versioning + IRELATIVE + GNU_RELRO
t25.description: .gnu.version/.gnu.version_r verneed records, R_X86_64_IRELATIVE ifunc relocations, PT_GNU_RELRO segment for glibc linkage

t24.subject: Rocq: build harness (_CoqProject + Makefile) compiles
t24.description: rocq-tests/_CoqProject, Makefile; all .v compile clean under coqc 9.1.0; add README mapping each theorem to QUAD.md section

t29.subject: peony -shared: emit ET_DYN shared objects
t29.description: Implement -shared in peony to emit ET_DYN shared objects (.so), required for cdylib/proc-macro crates that cargo install rebuilds. Subtasks: (1) parse -shared/-soname/-Bsymbolic; (2) skip crt injection, PT_INTERP, and entry-point resolution when shared; (3) export defined global symbols into .dynsym/.dynstr with correct st_shndx/st_value; (4) index exports in .hash + .gnu.hash buckets/chains; (5) emit DT_SONAME; (6) set e_type=ET_DYN with no PT_INTERP; (7) handle undefined imports + relocations (GLOB_DAT/JUMP_SLOT/RELATIVE) in shared context; (8) allow unresolved symbols (no error) since shared objs resolve at load. Verify: rustc --crate-type=cdylib links a .so that `file` reports as 'shared object' and that dlopen+dlsym resolves an exported fn.

t7.subject: Explore Wild linker codebase
t7.description: Use CodeGraph to explore /tmp/wild — find incremental linking implementation, symbol table, relocation scan, cache/diff logic, section layout. Extract key design patterns for peony.

t33.subject: cargo install --path . jfc with peony linker
t33.description: Run cargo install --path crates/jfc (or appropriate) for ~/RustProjects/active/jfc using peony as the linker via the new config. This rebuilds proc-macros (cdylib/.so — needs t29) and the final binary. Confirm the installed ~/.cargo/bin/jfc runs (jfc --version) and is peony-linked.

t15.subject: Implement PreHashed + parallel symbol scan
t15.description: Add PreHashed<K> type to peony-symbols, switch SymbolTable to use pre-hashed lookups per SPEC §5 / QUAD §2.3

t5.subject: Deep arXiv paper search
t5.description: Search arXiv/scholarly for more relevant papers: DWARF compression, persistent data structures, parallel symbol resolution, demand-driven compilation, whole-program optimization, debug info linkers

t28.subject: Rocq: NOVEL incremental-relink soundness + minimal-cut theorems
t28.description: rocq-tests/IncrementalSoundness.v — byte-identical incremental-relink soundness under capacity-stable invariant + minimal recomputation cut (genuinely novel per research/council); rocq-tests/RelocMonoid.v — relocation composition as partial commutative monoid

t13.subject: Add tests for new algorithms
t13.description: Write tests verifying S3-GC correctness, parallel emit determinism, incremental red-green patch-in-place, PreHashed lookups

t27.subject: Linker: .eh_frame_hdr + PT_GNU_EH_FRAME
t27.description: Synthesize sorted .eh_frame_hdr binary-search table from .eh_frame FDEs + PT_GNU_EH_FRAME segment; required for Rust unwinding

t12.subject: Write SPEC.md — complete implementation specification
t12.description: Translate QUAD.md theory into a concrete engineering spec: wire protocol, data formats, API contracts, phase pipeline, persistence schema, test oracle spec. Every data structure should have a formal invariant. Every phase should have a pre/postcondition.

t32.subject: peony -shared: ET_DYN headers, no crt/interp/entry
t32.description: Driver + layout changes: parse -shared/-soname/-Bsymbolic args; gate inject_crt_objects, PT_INTERP, and resolve_entry off when shared (e_entry=0 ok); set e_type=ET_DYN; emit DT_SONAME from -soname; allow undefined symbols without error (shared objects resolve imports at load time). Ensure DT_FLAGS/DT_FLAGS_1 appropriate (no DF_1_PIE for a .so).

t30.subject: peony -shared: runtime dlopen/dlsym validation
t30.description: Write a C harness that dlopen()s the peony-linked .so, dlsym()s an exported function, calls it, and checks the result. Also link a multi-symbol cdylib and verify all exports resolve. Confirms the .so is actually usable at runtime, not just structurally valid.

t19.subject: Fix premature done.store(true) in ws-deque workers
t19.description: Fix the termination signal in peony-layout gc_sections() and peony-emit write_section_data_parallel() so workers drain fully via stealing before exiting

t14.subject: Implement parallel reloc apply + TLB-aware emit
t14.description: Parallelize section copy and relocation application in peony-emit; implement TLB-aware overwrite-in-place (QUAD §7, SPEC Phase 8)
